author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XIX. 27.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then answered Peter and said unto Him, Behold, we have forsaken all, and followed Thee; what shall we have therefore?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: O blessed Peter; the rod? the net? the boat? the craft?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things dost thou tell me of, as all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, saith he, but not for display do I say these things, but in order that by this question I may bring in the multitude of the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since the Lord had said, “If thou wilt be perfect, sell that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in Heaven;”lest any one of the poor should say, What then? if I have no possessions, can I not be perfect?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Peter asks, that thou, the poor man, mayest learn, that thou art made in no respect inferior by this: Peter asks, that thou mayest not learn from Peter and doubt (for indeed he was imperfect as yet, and void of the Spirit), but that, having received the declaration from Peter’s Master, thou mayest be confident.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as we do (we make things our own often when speaking of the concerns of others), so did the apostle, when he put to Him this question in behalf of all the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since that at least he knew with certainty his own portion, is manifest from what had been said before; for he that had already received the keys of the Heavens, much more might feel confidence about the things hereafter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark also how exactly his reply is according to Christ’s demand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He had required of the rich man these two things, to give that he had to the poor, and to follow Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore he also expresses these two things, to forsake, and to follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For behold we have forsaken all,” saith he, “and have followed Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the forsaking was done for the sake of following, and the following was rendered easier by the forsaking, and made them feel confidence and joy touching the forsaking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Verily, I say unto you, that ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of Man shall sit on the throne of His glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then, one may say, shall Judas sit there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How, then, doth He say, “Ye shall sit on twelve thrones?” how shall the terms of the promise be fulfilled?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear how, and on what principle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is a law ordained of God, recited by Jeremiah, the prophet to the Jews, and in these words: “At what instant I shall speak a sentence concerning a nation and kingdom, to pluck up and destroy; if that nation turn from their evil deeds, I also will repent of the evils, which I thought to do unto them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And at what instant I shall speak concerning a nation and kingdom to build and to plant it; and if they do evil in my sight, that they obey not my voice, I also will repent of the good, which I said I would do unto them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the same custom do I observe with respect to the good things as well, saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though I spake of building up, should they show themselves unworthy of the promise, I will no longer do it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which sort of thing was done with respect to man upon his creation, “For the dread of you,” it is said, “and the fear of you shall be on the wild beasts,”and it came not to pass, for he proved himself unworthy of the sovereignty, even as did Judas also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in order that neither at the denunciations of punishment any men should despair and become more hardened, nor by the promises of good things be rendered causelessly more remiss, He remedies both these evils, by that which I have before mentioned, saying in this way: Though I should threaten, do not despair; for thou art able to repent, and to reverse the denunciation, like the Ninevites.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though I should promise any good thing, grow not remiss because of the promise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For shouldest thou appear unworthy, the fact of my having promised will not advantage thee, but will rather bring punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I promise thee being worthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore even then in His discourse with His disciples He did not promise to them simply, for neither did He say, “you,” only, but added, “which have followed me,” that He might both cast out Judas, and draw towards Him those that should come afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither to them only was it said, nor to Judas any more, when he had become unworthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now to the disciples He promised things to come, saying, “Ye shall sit on twelve thrones,” for they were now of a higher stamp, and sought after none of the things of the present world, but to the rest He promises also what are here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “every one,” He saith, “that hath forsaken brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, or house, for my name’s sake, shall receive an hundredfold in this world, and shall inherit eternal life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For lest any after having heard the word “ye,” should suppose this a thing peculiar to the disciples (I mean now the enjoying the greatest and first honors in the things to come), He extended the word, and spread the promise over the whole earth, and from the things present establishes the things to come also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to the disciples also at the beginning, when they were in a more imperfect state, He reasoned from the things present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He drew them from the sea, and took them from their trade, and commanded them to forsake the ships, He made mention not of Heaven, not of thrones, but of the things here, saying, “I will make you fishers of men;” but when He had wrought them to be of higher views, then after that He discourses of the things to come also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what is, “Judging the twelve tribes of Israel?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is, “condemning them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they are not surely to sit as judges, but like as He said the Queen of the South should condemn that generation, and the Ninevites shall condemn them; so now these also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He said not, the nations, and the world, but the tribes of Israel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since both the Jews alike and the apostles had been brought up under the same laws, and customs, and polity; when the Jews said, that for this cause they could not believe in Christ, because the law forbade to receive His commandments, by bringing forward these men, who had received the same law, and yet had believed, He condemns all those; like as even already He had said, “therefore they shall be your judges.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what great thing doth He promise them, it may be said, if what the Ninevites have and the Queen of the South, this these are to have also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the first place He had promised them many other things before this, and after this doth promise them, and this alone is not their reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides even in this He intimated by the way something more than these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For of those He simply said, The men of Nineveh shall rise up and condemn this gen eration,”and, “The Queen of the South shall condemn it;” but concerning these, not merely thus, but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “When the Son of Man shall sit upon the throne of His glory, then shall ye also sit upon twelve thrones,” saith He, declaring, that they also shall reign with Him, and partake of that glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if we suffer,” it is said, “we shall also reign with Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither do the thrones signify a sitting (in judgment), for He alone is the one that shall sit and judge, but honor and glory unspeakable did He intimate by the thrones.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To these then He spake of these things, but to all the rest of eternal life and an hundredfold here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if to the rest, much more to these too, both these things, and the things in this life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this surely came to pass; for when they had left a fishing rod and a net, they possessed with authority the substances of all, the prices of the houses and the lands, and the very bodies of the believers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For often did they choose even to be slain for their sake, as Paul also bears witness to many, when he saith, “If it had been possible ye would have plucked out your eyes, and given them to me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when He saith, “Every one who hath forsaken wife,” He saith not this, for marriages to be broken asunder for nought, but as He saith concerning one’s life, “He that loseth his life for my sake shall find it,”not that we should destroy ourselves, neither that while yet here we should part it from the body, but that we should prefer godliness to all things; this too He saith also with respect to wife and brethren.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He seems to me here to intimate also the persecutions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since there were many instances both of fathers urging their sons to ungodliness, and wives their husbands; when they command these things, saith He, let them be neither wives nor parents, even as Paul likewise said, “But if the unbelieving depart, let him depart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When He had then raised the spirit of all, and had persuaded them to feel confidence both with respect to themselves and to all the world, He added, that “Many that were first shall be last, and last first.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this although it be spoken also without distinction concerning many others likewise, it is spoken also concerning these men and concerning the Pharisees, who did not believe, even as before also He had said, “Many shall come from east and west and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob; but the children of the kingdom shall be cast out.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He adds also a parable, as training those who had fallen short to a great forwardness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the kingdom of Heaven,” He said, “is like to a man that is an householder, which went out early in the morning to hire laborers into his vineyard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when he had agreed with them for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And at the third hour he saw others standing idle, and to them too he said, Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And about the sixth and ninth hours he did likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And about the eleventh hour, he saw others standing idle, and saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they say unto him, No man hath hired us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He saith unto them, Go ye also into my vineyard, and whatsoever is right, ye shall receive.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the laborers, and give them their hire, beginning from the last unto the first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the first supposed that they should receive more, and they received likewise every man a penny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they had received it, they murmured against the good man of the house, saying, These last have wrought but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us that have borne the burden and heat of the day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But he answered one of them, and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong; didst thou not agree with me for a penny?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Take that thine is, and go thy way; I will give unto this last also, even as unto thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is thine eye evil, because I am good?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus the last shall be first, and the first last: for many are called, but few chosen.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is to us the intent of this parable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the beginning doth not harmonize with what is said at the end, but intimates altogether the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in the first part He shows all enjoying the same, and not some cast out, and some brought in; yet He Himself both before the parable and after the parable said the opposite thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “That the first shall be last, and the last first,” that is, before the very first, those not continuing first, but having become last.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that this is His meaning, He added, “Many are called, but few chosen,” so as doubly both to sting the one, and to soothe and urge on the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the parable saith not this, but that they shall be equal to them that are approved, and have labored much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For thou hast made them equal unto us,” it is said, “that have borne the burden and heat of the day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the meaning of the parable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is necessary to make this first clear, and then we shall clear up that other point.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By a vineyard He meaneth the injunctions of God and His commandments: by the time of laboring, the present life: by laborers, them that in different ways are called to the fulfillment of the injunctions: by early in the morning, and about the third and ninth and eleventh hours, them who at different ages have drawn near to God, and approved themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the question is this, whether the first having gloriously approved themselves, and having pleased God, and having throughout the whole day shone by their labors, are possessed by the basest feeling of vice, jealousy and envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they had seen them enjoying the same rewards, they say, “These last have wrought but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, that have borne the burden and heat of the day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in these words, when they are to receive no hurt, neither to suffer diminution as to their own hire, they were indignant, and much displeased at the good of others, which was proof of envy and jealousy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is yet more, the good man of the house in justifying himself with respect to them, and in making his defense to him that had said these things, convicts him of wickedness and the basest jealousy, saying, “Didst thou not agree with me for a penny?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Take that thine is, and go thy way; I will give unto the last even as unto thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is thine eye evil, because I am good?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is it which is to be established by these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in other parables also this self-same thing may be seen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the son who was approved is brought in, as having felt this self-same thing, when he saw his prodigal brother enjoying much honor, even more than himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as these enjoyed more by receiving first, so he in a greater degree was honored by the abundance of the things given him; and to these things he that was approved bears witness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then may we say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no one who is thus justifying himself, or blaming others in the kingdom of Heaven; away with the thought! for that place is pure from envy and jealousy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if when they are here the saints give their very lives for sinners, much more when they see them there in the enjoyment of these things, do they rejoice and account these to be blessings of their own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then did He so frame His discourse?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The saying is a parable, wherefore neither is it right to inquire curiously into all things in parables word by word,but when we have learnt the object for which it was composed, to reap this, and not to busy one’s self about anything further.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then was this parable thus composed? what is its object to effect?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To render more earnest them that are converted and become better men in extreme old age, and not to allow them to suppose they have a less portion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So it is for this cause He introduces also others displeased at their blessings, not to represent those men as pining or vexed, away with the thought! but to teach us that these have enjoyed such honor, as could even have begotten envy in others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which we also often do, saying, “Such a one blamed me, because I counted thee worthy of much honor,” neither having been blamed, nor wishing to slander that other, but hereby to show the greatness of the gift which this one enjoyed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore can it have been that He did not hire all at once?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As far as concerned Him, He did hire all; but if all did not hearken at once, the difference was made by the disposition of them that were called.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause, some are called early in the morning, some at the third hour, some at the sixth, some at the ninth, some at the eleventh, when they would obey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This Paul also declared when he said, “When it pleased Him, who separated me from my mother’s womb.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When did it please Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When he was ready to obey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He willed it even from the beginning, but because he would not have yielded, then it pleased Him, when Paul also was ready to obey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus also did He call the thief, although He was able to have called him even before, but he would not have obeyed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if Paul at the beginning would not have obeyed, much more the thief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if they say, “No man hath hired us,” in the first place as I said we must not be curious about all the points in the parables; but here neither is the good man of the house represented to say this, but they; but he doth not convict them, that he might drive them to perplexity, but might win them over.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that He called all, as far as lay in Him, from the first even the parable shows, saying, that “He went out early in the morning to hire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From everything then it is manifest to us, that the parable is spoken with reference to them who from earliest youth, and those who in old age and more tardily, lay hold on virtue; to the former, that they may not be proud, neither reproach those called at the eleventh hour; to the latter, that they may learn that it is possible even in a short time to recover all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since He had been speaking about earnestness, and the casting away of riches, and contempt of all one’s possessions, but this needed much vigor of mind and youthful ardor; in order to kindle in them a fire of love, and to give vigor to their will, He shows that it is possible even for men coming later to receive the hire of the whole day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He doth not say it thus, lest again He should make them proud, but he shows that the whole is of His love to man, and because of this they shall not fail, but shall themselves enjoy the unspeakable blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this chiefly is what it is His will to establish by this parable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if He adds, that, “So the last shall be first and the first last; for many are called, but few chosen,” marvel not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not as inferring it from the parable doth He say this, but His meaning is this, that like as this came to pass, so shall that come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For here indeed the first did not become last, but all received the same contrary to hope and expectation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as this result took place contrary to hope and contrary to expectation, and they that came before were equalled by them that followed, so shall that also come to pass which is more than this, and more strange, I mean, that the last should come to be even before the first, and that the first should be after these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that that is one thing, and this another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He seems to me to say these things, darkly hinting at the Jews, and amongst the believers at those who at first shone forth, but afterwards neglected virtue, and fell back; and those others again that have risen from vice, and have shot beyond many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we see such changes taking place both with respect to faith and practice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I entreat you let us use much diligence both to stand in the right faith, and to show forth an excellent life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For unless we add also a life suitable to our faith, we shall suffer the extremest punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this the blessed Paul showed even from times of old, when he said, that “They did all eat the same spiritual meat, and did all drink the same spiritual drink:” and added, that they were not saved; “for they were overthrown in the wilderness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Christ declared it even in the evangelists, when He brought in some that had cast out devils and prophesied, and are led away to punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And all His parables also, as that of the virgins, that of the net, that of the thorns, that of the tree not bringing forth fruit, demand virtue in our works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For concerning doctrines He discourses seldom, for neither doth the subject need labor, but of life often or rather everywhere, for the war about this is continual, wherefore also so is the labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I speak of the whole code.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even a part of it overlooked brings upon one great evils; as, for instance, almsgiving overlooked casts into hell them that have come short in it; and yet this is not the whole of virtue, but a part thereof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nevertheless both the virgins were punished for not having this, and the rich man was for this cause tormented, and they that have not fed the hungry, are for this condemned with the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, not to revile is a very small part of it, nevertheless this too casts out them that have not attained to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For he that saith to his brother, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, even continence itself is a part, but nevertheless, without this no one shall see the Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Follow peace,” it is said, “and holiness,without which no man shall see the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And humility too in like manner is a part of virtue; but nevertheless though any one should fulfill other good works, but have not attained to this, he is unclean with God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is manifest from the Pharisee, who though abounding with numberless good works, by this lost all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I have also something more than these things to say again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, that not only one of them overlooked shuts Heaven against us, but though it be done, yet not in due perfection and abundance, it produces the selfsame effect again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the Scribes and Pharisees, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that though thou give alms, but not more than they, thou shalt not enter in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how much did they bestow in alms? one may ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this very thing, I am minded to say now, that they who do not give may be roused to give, and they that give may not pride themselves, but may make increase of their gifts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then did they give?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A tenth of all their possessions, and again another tenth, and after this a third, so that they almost gave away the third part, for three-tenths put together make up this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with these, first fruits, and first born, and other things besides, as, for instance, the offerings for sins, those for purification, those at feasts, those in the jubilee,those by the cancelling of debts, and the dismissals of servants, and the lendings that were clear of usury.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if he who gave the third part of his goods, or rather the half (for those being put together with these are the half), if then he who is giving the half, achieves no great thing, he who doth not bestow so much as the tenth, of what shall he be worthy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With reason He said, “There are few that be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not, then, despise the care of our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if one portion of it despised brings so great a destruction, when on every hand we are subject to the sentence of condemnation, how shall we escape the punishment? and what manner of penalty shall we not suffer? and what manner of hope of salvation have we, one may ask, if each of the things we have numbered threatens us with hell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I too say this; nevertheless, if we give heed we may be saved, preparing the medicines of almsgiving, and attending to our wounds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For oil does not so strengthen a body, as benevolence at once strengthens a soul, and makes it invincible to all and impregnable to the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wheresoever he may seize us, his hold then slips, this oil not suffering his grasp to fix on our back.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this oil therefore let us anoint ourselves continually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is the cause of health, and a supply of light, and a source of cheerfulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But such a one,” thou wilt say, “hath talents of gold so many and so many, and gives away nothing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is that to thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus shalt thou appear more worthy of admiration, when in poverty thou art more munificent than he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was on this ground Paul marvelled at the Macedonians, not because they gave, but because even though they were in poverty they gave.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Look not then at these, but at the common Teacher of all, who “had not where to lay His head.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why, you say, doth not this and that person do so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not judge another, but deliver thyself from the charge against thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since the punishment is greater when thou at the same time blamest others, and thyself doest not, when judging other men, thou art again thyself also subject to the same judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if even them who do right He permits not to judge others, much more will He not permit offenders.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not therefore judge others, neither let us look to others who are taking their ease, but unto Jesus, and from thence let us draw our examples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why! have I been thy benefactor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why! did I redeem thee, that thou lookest to me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is another who hath bestowed these things on thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou let go thy Master, and look unto thy fellow-servant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Heardest thou not Him saying, “Learn of me, for I am meek and lowly in heart?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “He that would be first amongst you, let him be servant of all:” and again, “Even as the Son of Man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And after these things again, lest taking offense at them who are remiss amongst thy fellow-servants, thou continue in contemptuousness; to draw thee off from that, He saith, “I have made myself an example to you, that as I have done, ye should do also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But hast thou no teacher of virtue amongst those persons that are with thee, neither such a one as to lead thee on to these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: More abundant then will be the praise, the commendation greater, when not even being supplied with teachers thou hast become one to be marvelled at.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is possible, nay very easy, if we be willing: and this they show, who first duly performed these things, as for instance, Noah, Abraham, Melchizedeck, Job, and all the men like them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To them it is needful to look every day, and not unto these, whom ye never cease emulating, and passing about their names in your assemblies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing else do I hear you saying everywhere, but such words as these; “Such a one has bought so many acres of land; such a one is rich, he is building.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou stare, O man, at what is without?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou look to others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou art minded to look to others, look to them that do their duty, to them that approve themselves, to them that carefully fulfill the law, not to those that have become offenders, and are in dishonor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou look to these, thou wilt gather hence many evil things, falling into remissness, into pride, into condemnation of others; but if thou reckon over them that do right, thou wilt lead thyself on unto humility, unto diligence, unto compunction, unto the blessings that are beyond number.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear what the Pharisee suffered, because he let pass them that do right, and looked to him that had offended; hear and fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how David became one to be marvelled at, because he looked to his ancestors that were noted for virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For I am a stranger,” saith he, “and a sojourner, as all my fathers were.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this man, and all that are like him, let pass them that had sinned, and thought of those who had approved themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This do thou also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou art not set to judge of the negligences of which others have been guilty, nor to inquire into the sins which others are committing; thou art required to do judgment on thyself, not on others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if we judged ourselves,” it is said, “we should not be judged, but when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou hast reversed the order, of thyself requiring no account of offenses great or small, but being strict and curious about the offenses of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us no more do this, but leaving off this disorderly way, let us set up a tribunal in ourselves for the sins committed by ourselves, becoming ourselves accusers, and judges, and executioners for our offenses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if it be thy will to be busy about the things of other men also, busy thyself about their good works, not their sins, that both by the memory of our negligences and by our emulation for the good works they have done, and by setting before ourselves the judgment-seat from which no prayers can deliver, wounded each day by our conscience as by a kind of goad,we may lead ourselves on to humility, and a greater diligence, and attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ; with whom be to the Father, together with the Holy Ghost, glory, might, honor, now and always, and world without end.
